marketFocus: Trump's Iran deadline at 02:00 FFT (Wed) is the dominant theme. WTI above $116/bbl, Brent above $109/bbl. Trump threatened to destroy all Iranian bridges and power plants within 4 hours if no deal. Iran rejected US 45-day ceasefire proposal with a 10-point counter. Two Israeli sources say 'very low chance of agreement.' Strikes continue: Saudi Jubail industrial hub hit (largest petrochemical complex in Middle East), Bahrain, Kuwait, Baghdad, UAE data centers targeted.

NFP Friday Apr 3 MASSIVE BEAT: +178K (vs +60K exp). Unemployment Rate: 4.3% (vs 4.4% exp). ISM Services PMI Monday: 54.0 (miss on headline) but Prices Paid surged to 70.7 (from 63.0) — stagflation confirmed. Employment component dropped to 45.2. Fed Hammack: 'Inflation is bright orange.' Goolsbee: 'Going from orange to red amid tariffs and energy price shocks.' USD stagflation framework fully intact.

Japan Household Spending: -2.1% YoY (vs -0.5% exp) — MISS. 30yr JGB auction b/c fell to 3.12x (prev 3.66x) — weak demand. USD/JPY hit 159.83 on Monday. JPY pressured by higher oil prices and rising UST yields. Canada Services PMI: 47.2 — 5th consecutive month of contraction. Key events today: USD Core Durable Goods 13:30 FFT, CAD Ivey PMI 15:00 FFT. Trump Iran deadline 02:00 FFT Wed.

USD.tone: NFP +178K Massive Beat + ISM Prices Paid 70.7 — Stagflation Framework Confirmed, DXY Firmer
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. Friday's NFP was a massive beat: +178K (vs +60K exp), unemployment fell to 4.3%. ISM Services Prices Paid surged to 70.7 (from 63.0) — the highest since the post-COVID inflation peak. Stagflation framework is fully confirmed: strong labour market + surging input costs + oil above $116/bbl. Fed Hammack said 'inflation is bright orange' and Goolsbee said it is 'going from orange to red.' DXY firmed marginally on Monday. Risk-off ahead of Trump's 02:00 FFT deadline supports USD haven demand. Core Durable Goods Orders at 13:30 FFT today is the key data catalyst. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: NFP MASSIVE BEAT: +178K (vs +60K exp); Unemployment Rate fell to 4.3% — labour market strong
ISM Services Prices Paid surged to 70.7 (from 63.0) — stagflation signal confirmed
Fed Hammack: 'Inflation is bright orange'; Goolsbee: 'going from orange to red'
DXY firmer on risk-off ahead of Trump's 02:00 FFT Iran deadline
Core Durable Goods Orders at 13:30 FFT today — key USD catalyst

EUR.tone: ECB Hawkish Repricing Intact but CPI Miss Caps Upside — EUR/USD Holding 1.3200 Area
EUR.summary: EUR is Neutral today. EUR/USD is holding the 1.3200 area but lacks directional momentum. The Eurozone CPI Flash last week missed expectations (2.5% vs 2.8% exp) — the energy shock has not yet filtered through into broader prices, reinforcing the ECB's 'wait and see' approach. ECB Radev said the ECB 'must be ready to act if inflation persists' but added it is 'too early to say if April rate hike is needed.' Bund supply this week (EUR 6.5bln Tue-Wed) weighs on EUR. European markets were closed for Easter Monday — light newsflow. Eurozone Services PMI Final today. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: EUR/USD holding 1.3200 area — light newsflow after Easter Monday closures
ECB Radev: 'must be ready to act if inflation persists; too early to say if April hike needed'
Eurozone CPI Flash missed last week (2.5% vs 2.8% exp) — ECB wait-and-see reinforced
Bund supply EUR 6.5bln Tue-Wed — weighs on EUR via fixed income pressure
Eurozone Services PMI Final today — watch for any upside surprise

GBP.tone: GBP/USD Retaining 1.3200 Handle — UK Newsflow Light, BOE Repricing Intact
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. GBP/USD is retaining the 1.3200 handle with very few catalysts for the UK. UK markets were closed for Easter Monday — light newsflow. BOE hawkish repricing (~81bps tightening priced) remains intact but no fresh catalyst to drive GBP directionally. UK Services PMI Final today is the key domestic event. The London City financial figures warned that Brussels banking rules could impact lenders in the context of the UK-EU reset — a mild headwind. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: GBP/USD retaining 1.3200 handle — UK markets closed Easter Monday, light newsflow
BOE hawkish repricing (~81bps tightening priced) intact — no fresh catalyst needed
UK Services PMI Final today — key domestic event for GBP direction
London City warned Brussels banking rules could impact lenders in UK-EU reset context
No fresh GBP catalyst — pure risk sentiment and USD dynamics driving GBP/USD

JPY.tone: USD/JPY Hit 159.83 — JPY Pressured by Higher Oil, Rising UST Yields, Weak Household Spending
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY hit 159.83 on Monday — JPY is under pressure from multiple headwinds. Japan Household Spending missed badly: -2.1% YoY (vs -0.5% exp). The 30yr JGB auction showed weak demand (b/c fell to 3.12x from 3.66x, average yield 3.697% from 3.398%). Higher oil prices are a structural negative for Japan as a net energy importer. Rising UST yields (10yr at 4.333%) widen the US-Japan yield differential and pressure JPY. Japanese PM Takaichi is 'strongly urging Iran to allow Hormuz safe passage' — Japan's energy vulnerability is acute. BOJ verbal intervention at 160 is expected but not acting. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = NO alignment (mismatch).
drivers: USD/JPY hit 159.83 Monday — JPY pressured by oil and rising UST yields
Japan Household Spending MISSED: -2.1% YoY (vs -0.5% exp) — domestic demand weak
30yr JGB auction: b/c fell to 3.12x (prev 3.66x) — weak demand, yields rising
Higher oil prices structural negative for Japan — net energy importer, Hormuz closure acute
BOJ verbal intervention expected at 160 but not acting — JPY vulnerable to further downside

AUD.tone: Antipodeans Pulled Back After Easter Monday — Cautious Ahead of Trump Deadline, No Catalyst
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. Antipodeans pulled back after Easter Monday's mild cyclical outperformance. AUD/USD topped at 0.6938 on Monday but faded as markets turned cautious ahead of Trump's 02:00 FFT deadline. China PBoC set USD/CNY mid-point weaker than expected (6.8854 vs 6.8773 exp) — a mild negative for AUD via the China channel. Xi Jinping called for a 'new energy system' as the Iran war rocks the global economy — China is accelerating energy independence planning. No domestic AUD catalyst today. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: AUD/USD topped at 0.6938 Monday but faded — cautious ahead of Trump Iran deadline
PBoC set USD/CNY weaker than expected (6.8854 vs 6.8773 exp) — mild negative for AUD
Xi calls for 'new energy system' — China accelerating energy independence, commodity demand risk
No domestic AUD catalyst today — pure risk sentiment and China proxy
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

CAD.tone: Canada Services PMI 47.2 — 5th Consecutive Month of Contraction, Ivey PMI at 15:00 FFT
CAD.summary: CAD is Bearish today. Canada Services PMI came in at 47.2 (from 46.5 prev) — the 5th consecutive month of contraction. Input costs rose sharply (highest in 9 months) driven by fuel and transportation. Employment declined again. Despite oil above $116/bbl, CAD is not benefiting as the structural weakness in the domestic services sector dominates. Ivey PMI at 15:00 FFT today is the key domestic catalyst — a miss would compound CAD weakness. Canada's energy sector is being disrupted by the Iran war (Hormuz closure affects global shipping costs). Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = NO alignment (mismatch).
drivers: Canada Services PMI: 47.2 — 5th consecutive month of contraction; demand still falling
Input costs rose sharply (9-month high) — fuel, transport, labour costs surging
Employment declined again — labour market softening in services sector
Ivey PMI at 15:00 FFT today — miss would compound CAD bearish pressure
Structural domestic weakness offsetting oil price support from Hormuz closure

CHF.tone: Haven Flows Prefer USD — SNB Cap Intact, CHF Firmed Slightly vs USD but Structural Bearish
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Haven flows are preferring USD over CHF in the current risk-off environment. CHF firmed slightly against USD on Monday as the dollar lagged peers, but the structural bearish case remains intact. SNB intervention cap limits CHF appreciation. No Swiss data today. USD/CHF is stabilizing. The ECB CPI miss last week reduces EUR/CHF upside pressure — EUR/CHF rangebound near 0.9100. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: Haven flows preferring USD over CHF in risk-off environment — USD/CHF stabilizing
SNB intervention cap intact — limits CHF appreciation against EUR and USD
CHF firmed slightly vs USD Monday but structural bearish case remains intact
ECB CPI miss reduces EUR/CHF upside pressure — pair rangebound near 0.9100
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅

NZD.tone: RBNZ Decision at 03:00 FFT Wed — NZD/USD Faded After Easter Monday Bounce, High-Beta Risk-Off
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. NZD/USD hit 0.5730 on Easter Monday but faded as markets turned cautious ahead of Trump's deadline. The RBNZ rate decision is at 03:00 FFT Wednesday — a hold at 2.25% is expected but any dovish guidance would pressure NZD further. NZD is a high-beta risk proxy and suffers in risk-off environments. No domestic NZD catalyst today beyond the RBNZ overnight warning. RBNZ stagflation warning (energy shock + weak growth) remains intact. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ✅.
drivers: NZD/USD hit 0.5730 Easter Monday but faded — cautious ahead of Trump Iran deadline
RBNZ Rate Decision at 03:00 FFT Wed — hold at 2.25% expected; dovish guidance = NZD risk
High-beta risk proxy — NZD underperforms in risk-off environments
RBNZ stagflation warning intact — energy shock raises inflation while weighing on growth
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅
